title: The Cold War
description: This exercise will cover the context of the Cold War as well as the causes and effects of it. This exercise will have two types based on the themes GOV and CDI and each type will scale 5 items.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>...For those reasons I think we may approach calmly and with good heart the problem of how to deal with Russia. As to how this approach should be made, I only wish to advance, by way of conclusion, the following comments:
>
>...We must formulate and put forward for other nations a much more positive and constructive picture of the sort of world we would like to see...It is not enough to urge people to develop political processes similar to our own. Many foreign peoples, in Europe at least, are tired and frightened by experiences of the past, and are less interested in abstract freedom than in security…We should be better able than the Russians to give them this. And unless we do, the Russians certainly will.

Source: George Kennen, “The Long Telegram” 1946, USSR. 

**Which of the following policy developments were directly influenced by the telegram that was excerpted above?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Marshall Plan
the dropping of the atomic bomb
the United Nations Charter
the spread of communism to China

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best represents an example of American postwar economic policy?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: investing in the rebuilding of former Axis Powers to secure necessary markets
using American businesses to control to shape economic dynamics in Latin America
granting broad economic aid to European nations
containing the spread of communism through direct military action

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following could a historian use as evidence of the worsening relationship between the United States and the Soviet Union in the years following World War II?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Soviet Union and its allies refused economic aid from the Marshall Plan.
Communism spread to China in the late 1940s.
Russia used militarization to promote industrial development in the postwar economy.
The United States conducted nuclear tests in nearby Alaska to send a message to the Soviet Union.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the broad historical context of the Cold War from 1945 until 1970 CE?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: European empires collapsed after World War II, creating new states across Africa and Asia.
Germany accepted full blame for the war and was plunged into a deep economic depression.
Most countries adopted strict isolationist policies in an effort to avoid another World War in the future.
Rejections of past imperialism led to a more economically developed global landscape.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best explains the impact that Western European and Japanese economic growth had on the people of Africa, Asia, and Latin America in the years following World War II?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The demand for cash crops led to the exploitation of workers.
The import of raw materials to Western Europe and Japan benefited those who exported them.
More refined raw materials were needed for the new industrial boom, which decreased demand for Asian and African crops.
Cash crop production decreased in Asia and Africa but increased in Latin America.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best explains a continuity in the global economic structure in the time period from 1900 to the present?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Inequalities between rich and poor nations remained unchanged as imperialism gave way to neo-colonialism.
Political power remained concentrated in the hands of European nations.
Countries continued to protect their domestic economies through government control, tariffs, and other protectionist policies.
The economies of former colonies still rely heavily on manufacturing and service industries.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following events best exemplify how Cold War foreign policies impacted nations trying to achieve economic independence?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: In Iran, the American government used the CIA to overthrow a nationalist leader and install a dictator.
In Egypt, the Suez Canal was successfully nationalized, though Britain, France, and Israel opposed this militarily.
In India, the Green Revolution happened as a result of technological advancements.
In China, the Great Leap Forward aimed at rapidly industrializing the nation.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.** 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following changes in Western European and American communities is depicted in the image above?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Photograph of an aerial view of hundreds of houses all shaped and spaced the same.
options.caption: Source: Aerial view of Levittown, Pennsylvania. Circa 1959. Public domain.

choices.content: the growth of suburbs
the rise of centralized urban planning
the decline of home ownership
increasing urbanization

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following developments contributed most directly to the rise of the Cold War?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Communist societies believed in a command economy, while capitalist systems relied on a market economy.
The United States and the Soviet Union were allies during World War II, cooperating to defeat the Axis Powers.
The United States landed the first humans on the moon in 1969, marking a significant milestone in the Space Race.
The Treaty of Versailles redrew the map of Europe and created new national boundaries.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following developments undermines the foreign policy principle of self-determination that was meant to be upheld in global politics after World War II?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the United States’ involvement in Vietnam
China’s acceptance of communism
India’s policy of non-alignment
the decline of the British Empire

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>From Stettin in the Baltic to Trieste in the Adriatic an iron curtain has descended across the Continent. Behind that line lie all the capitals of the ancient states of Central and Eastern Europe. Warsaw, Berlin, Prague, Vienna, Budapest, Belgrade, Bucharest and Sofia, all these famous cities and the populations around them lie in what I must call the Soviet sphere, and all are subject in one form or another, not only to Soviet influence but to a very high and, in some cases, increasing measure of control from Moscow... 

Source: Winston Churchhill, “The Sinews of Peace” (also known as “The ‘Iron Curtain’ Speech”), 1946, Missouri, United States of America. 

**Which of the following best reflects the effects of the developments described in the excerpt above?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Trade networks and communities were split, and cultural exchange became difficult.
Direct military action between those in Eastern and Western Europe became inevitable.
The United States instituted the Marshall Plan to help Western Europe recover from the war.
Russian became a common language in the Soviet Union.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following developments best represents opposition to existing political and economic orders during the Cold War?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Non-Aligned Movement
the emergence of communism
the rise of environmentalism
protests against free trade
